Bus Timings

Updated 88 Bus Timings Dubai Internet City Metro Seaside to Deira City Center Bus Station

June 11, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

The 88 Bus route in Dubai is an essential public transport link, connecting Dubai Internet City Seaside Metro Station to Deira City Center Bus Station.

Located in the bustling area of Dubai Internet City, it serves as a key access point for numerous tech companies, media organizations, and educational institutions in the vicinity. This route is popular among commuters and travelers due to its comprehensive coverage of key areas in Dubai, making it a reliable option for daily transportation. Contents

88 Bus Timetable

The 88 Bus operates daily, providing frequent service throughout the day. The schedule is designed to accommodate the busy lifestyles of Dubai’s residents and visitors. Typically, buses run every 15-20 minutes during peak hours and every 30 minutes during off-peak times.

From Dubai Internet City Metro Seaside From Deira City Center Bus Station
05:40 06:55
06:00 07:15
06:20 07:35
06:40 07:55

07:10 08:25

Download PDF Bus 88

To view the full schedule, you can visit the official RTA website or follow this link to access the 88 Bus Timings. For the timetable from Dubai Internet City Metro Seaside to Deira City Center Bus Station, you can find the details below:

88 Bus Route

The 88 Bus route starts from Dubai Internet City Seaside Metro Station and ends at Deira City Center Bus Station. Here are some of the main stops along the route:

  1. Dubai Internet City Seaside Metro Station: The starting point, located in the heart of Dubai’s technology and business hub.

  2. Knowledge Village: Serving the educational district with numerous universities and institutions.

  3. Dubai Media City: Home to several media organizations and offices.

4.

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

5.

  1. Jumeirah Beach Residence (JBR): A popular residential and tourist area.

  2. Jumeirah Beach Park: Providing access to one of Dubai’s most loved beaches.

  3. Al Wasl Road: A major thoroughfare connecting various parts of the city.

  4. Satwa: A culturally rich neighborhood with a blend of old and new Dubai.

  5. Al Karama: Known for its shopping centers and diverse dining options.

  6. Al Maktoum Bridge: Offering views of the Dubai Creek and connecting the old and new parts of the city.

  7. Deira City Center Metro Station: A major commercial hub with a large shopping mall.

  8. Deira City Center Bus Station: The final stop, providing connections to various parts of the city and beyond.

Some other Dubai bus routes you can explore include the 13A, which passes through Al Qusais and Gold Souq, as well as the C18, which runs between Shaikh Rashid Colony and Lamcy Plaza.## Landmarks Along the Route

Traveling on the 88 Bus offers passengers a glimpse of some of Dubai’s most famous landmarks and areas:- Palm Jumeirah: The route passes close to this iconic island, providing views of luxury resorts and residences.

  • Dubai Marina: Known for its stunning skyline and waterfront, it’s a highlight of the journey.
  • Jumeirah Beach: Passengers can enjoy views of the beautiful beaches and the Arabian Gulf.
  • Burj Al Arab: While not directly on the route, the bus passes close enough to offer a distant view of this iconic hotel.
  • Dubai Creek: Crossing the Al Maktoum Bridge provides a scenic view of the creek and the surrounding areas.
  • Deira City Center: The route ends at this major commercial and retail hub, perfect for shopping and dining.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: June 11, 2025.

Written by Al Khaleej

Al Khaleej is the lead editor at Dubai Time — covering UAE transport, banking, visa services, and city guides. Trusted by residents and expats across the Emirates for accurate, up-to-date local information.
